Review Title: Brand New iPhone 17 Pro Max With Visible Dead Pixel ľ Replacement Denied Despite Proof
Review Description: The media could not be loaded.I purchased a brand new iPhone 17 Pro Max from Amazon, and within days of delivery the device started showing a visible red/dead pixel issue with intermittent flickering. The defect is clearly visible to the naked eye and appears across multiple screens and colors.I immediately followed the correct process and visited an Apple Authorized Service Center. The issue was officially recorded in their service report and even captured on video. However, after keeping the phone for two days, the service center verbally denied the issue and refused to issue a defective certificate, which Amazon requires for replacement.Because of this, I am now running out of my replacement window, despite the product being defective from the beginning. I had to take leave from work, visit the service center multiple times, and still ended up helpless.This is a premium flagship device, and such a defect on a brand new phone is unacceptable. Even more disappointing is how difficult it has been to get a fair replacement after following all official steps.I expected much better quality checks and post-sale support for a product of this price.Buyers beware: Please check your device very carefully immediately after delivery and be prepared for a long struggle if you receive a defective unit.Read more

I am reviewing this phone after a few days of usage.This is a compromised or cut-down version of the normal 5G phones and hence it's available for less than 10K INR.The built-in hardware test is removed. Review Title: Overall you can should buy realme 15x , or u can buy this also but i suggest realme 15x .
Review Description: First of all, I would like to talk about my Realme Narzo 80 Pro 5G. Overall, it is a very good phone in the 20ľ22k INR price range. I have been using this phone since 17 August 2025, and today is 28 November 2025Śalmost 4 months. The gaming performance is very good. The camera quality is also very good in bright environments such as sunlight or outdoors, especially when taking photos of people. Nature photography is also decent. Also sound quality is also good , i'm reading some negative comments , but i want to tell to whomever reading this review , The phone has dual stereo speaker support also , with 300% voice boost .I have the 8 GB RAM + 128 GB storage variant. I strongly suggest buying the 256 GB storage variant because in the 128 GB version, a large portion of the storage is taken by the systemŚaround 30ľ40 GB. So, you only get about 60ľ70 GB of usable space, which is quite low for modern usage. Even though the phone is advertised as 128 GB, you donĺt actually get the full 128 GB.In terms of performance, I did not face any heating issues. The phone stays cool during normal and even heavy tasks. It only heats slightly while charging, which is completely normal for fast-charging phones. The connectivity is also very good, with strong Wi-Fi and Bluetooth performance. Storage transfer speeds are fast as well, thanks to UFS 3.1 storage.I also appreciate the battery capacity, which is quite good for modern phones (6000 mAh). The battery lasts around 20ľ21 hours with moderate usage such as YouTube, Instagram Reels, or studying on the PW app. However, I have one issue with the screen. Although the display is very bright, easy to use in sunlight, and has excellent color quality, I would advise avoiding phones with curved displays. If a curved screen breaks after dropping on a cemented road or anywhere else, it is very difficult to find a duplicate replacement screen in the market.Additionally, you cannot expand the memory using an SD card, and there is no audio jack on this phone. On the positive side, the 80W charging is amazingŚit charges the phone up to 25% in just 10 minutes and more than 60% in under 30 minutes.Overall, the phone is very good, but I still recommend checking out the Realme 15x. It offers almost the same features and is even better in some aspects because it doesnĺt have a curved display. The Realme 15x also comes with 60W SUPERVOOC charging and a massive 7000 mAh battery, which is extremely impressive and lasts even longer.Read more

Review Title: great daily driver, with one flaw
Review Description: i have a lot of phones, as amazon and flipkart already know. but, i do NOT use my mid/high end phones as my daily drivers. my gaming phones are stuck at home and have almost never seen the world outside my house. i will also tell you why i don't use my mid/high end phones outside - because i tend to drop them, crack them, etc.but sometime in august/september i realized that i was doing so many things on my daily use phone that i needed a decent mid range phone. and that is when the search began. i had zeroed in on 4 different mid range phones, but i decided to settle on this one.my main criteria for choosing a new phone was that it had to be decently powerful (able to create a presentation, in the worst of the cases), and MORE IMPORTANTLY, this new phone had to be very light weight.this phone, the iqoo z10r fit the bill in every way, but one (wait for it).the other phone i almost bought, but didn't really, was the tecno pova curve. that's a slim, light weight phone with a decent chipset (7300). it lost in the looks department.but this phone had a lot of things going for it: a solid chipset (7400), great cameras (for a mid range phone, especially with the vivo legacy), fairly slim, and fairly light weight.i have been using this phone as my daily driver for the past few months now, and the iqoo z10r has NOT disappointed in most departments, spare one: this phone is wide! had this phone been a little more wide... this phone would have been a square! holding the phone in a single hand is NOT easy because of how wide the phone is.i am not a fan of the camera bump either. and the light... again, i am not a fan of it.the front camera quality is amazing. rear cameras... i am not so sure. they are okay. my low/mid range infinix, redmi and realme phones probably take equally good, if not better pictures with their rear cameras, when compared to this phone's rear cameras.another big problem: i think this phone could get damaged quite easily given how thin it is. just an hour back i was getting out of my friend's car, and my phone was in between my legs, or maybe i was sitting on it, and the phone fell on a concrete road, as i exited the vehicle. for a minute i thought: there goes my 20k. luckily i had bought a decently tough cover for the phone, so the phone survived (i hope). let's see... we will know if this phone can actually survive a few feet falls onto heavy concrete streets.i have played multiple games on this phone - bgmi, racing games, some low end games, etc, and they all run smoothly. my ancient redmi k50 supports smooth + extreme+, but this phone supports only smooth + extreme. doesn't make much of a difference for me as i am a casual bgmi warrior.overall, this is a good phone for the price, if you can overlook the fact that it is VERY WIDE!Read more

I recently used the Boult X Mustang Dyno earbuds and overall, they are a good option in the budget segment.The design is one of the best things about these earbuds. Review Title: Bought it for its battery back-up but got more
Review Description: Let me start with the elephant in the room claim - 24 hours battery back up from buds alone. I received this TWS around 6:45 in the evening, took about a couple  minutes to check the box contents and make sure the TWS was in perfect condition. Paired them with my phone only to find out the earbuds had 100% charge, so I started using them. I initiated my "testing" at 7 p.m. with The Jurassic Park (1993) at about 80% volume. 2 hours later, the buds had ~93% charge left. I said, "Challenge accepted!", and then watched the sequel, The Lost World (1997). Another 2 hours and some odd minutes later, the time was 11:10 p.m., the left bud showed 86% battery and the right was at 85%. At this point, I could see myself losing the battle, but I didn't wanna give up. I thought to myself, "Maybe if I tried harder, I'd win." But time, as always, wasn't on my side. It was 11:20 p.m., the silence of the night could be heard loudly and the fatigue of the day was catching up to me, when suddenly, an idea popped into my head. I rushed over to YouTube, searched for a 10 hour heavy metal music video, hit the play button, increased the earbuds volume to 100%, and then put both my phone and the buds on the bedside table grinning to myself thinking, "Yeah, survive that.", and called it a night. The morning came and as my vision cleared up to show 7:20 a.m. on the clock, I reached out to my phone, assuredly and confidently convinced, I had won the war.I didn't. The right bud was at 41% and the left at 45%. At this juncture, I gladly accepted my defeat and decided to write this story, a story of a brave TWS, a story that will never be in any history books, a story where the eternal TWS won against its sadistic owner.As for the rest, I will keep it brief;In-ear Detection: Works very well, no complaints.Sound Quality: Signature mode is too bass-y, not for me, for sure. Balance mode does the job and it does it well.Call quality: THE BEST. Tested against Boat Rockers 255 Neo, Boult Audio X50, Dizo GoPods Neo, Realme T100.Build Quality: Buds good. Case lid, when closed, has a slight wobble to it. Everything else is upto the mark.One final remark would be in-ear fitting, and thus, noise isolation. I find these buds to provide noise isolation as good as Dizo Gopods Neo with its 25Db ANC mode on. It almost feels like they were custom made for my ears. But do keep in mind that this can and will differ from person to person.Thank you for your time and I hope I didn't waste it.Read more

Review Title: The Ultimate Disappointment: A Scathing Review of the Worst Earbuds Iĺve Ever Used
Review Description: The media could not be loaded.Let me preface this review by saying Iĺve tested countless earbuds over the years, ranging from budget-friendly options to premium audiophile gear. Never in my life have I encountered a product as fundamentally flawed, poorly designed, and outright infuriating as these earbuds. If youĺre considering purchasing these for gaming purposes, casual listening, or even as a backup pairŚSTOP. Save your money, your sanity, and your ears. From the abysmal mic functionality to the laughable sound quality, and the build quality that feels like it was engineered by someone whoĺs never held a pair of earbuds before, this product is a masterclass in failure. Letĺs break down why these earbuds are an insult to the very concept of functionality, clarity, and performance.1. Mic Functionality: A Joke for Gaming PurposesLetĺs start with the mic functionality, a feature thatĺs absolutely critical for gaming purposes or even casual Zoom calls. If youĺre buying earbuds to communicate with teammates, stream, or participate in online meetings, prepare to be humiliated. The microphone picks up every conceivable noise except your voice. Background chatter, the faint hum of your refrigerator, even your dog barking three rooms awayŚall of it is crystal clear. But your actual voice? Muffled, distorted, and buried under a layer of static that makes you sound like youĺre speaking through a tin can underwater.During a critical raid in my favorite MMO, my teammates repeatedly asked, ôAre you chewing gravel? Why does your mic sound like a broken walkie-talkie?ö When I switched to these earbudsĺ mic functionality, the response was unanimous: ôNever use these again.ö For gaming purposes, where clear communication is non-negotiable, this mic is a liability. Even after tweaking settings, updating drivers, and screaming into the microphone like a madman, the clarity never improved. Itĺs almost impressive how a company could engineer a microphone this bad.2. Gaming Purpose? More Like ôGame OveröSpeaking of gaming purposes, these earbuds fail on every front. Latency is so severe that actions in-gameŚlike gunfire or footstepsŚare delayed by a full second, making competitive play impossible. Imagine hearing an enemyĺs shotgun blast after youĺve already been respawned. The lack of clarity in spatial audio means you canĺt distinguish between sounds coming from your left or right, a death sentence in shooters or horror games.But the real kicker? The performance of these earbuds under load is laughable. After just 30 minutes of use, they overheat to the point of discomfort, and the audio begins to crackle like a campfire. For a product marketed toward gaming purposes, the complete disregard for basic functionality is staggering.3. Functionality: A Textbook Example of ôHow Not to Design EarbudsöThe functionality of these earbuds is so counterintuitive, it feels like a prank. The touch controls are hypersensitive yet unresponsiveŚa paradox that defies logic. Adjusting the volume? Prepare for a 50/50 chance of either muting the audio entirely or triggering your voice assistant. The companion app, which promises to ôenhance your experience,ö is a glitchy mess that crashes more often than a toddlerĺs toy car.Even basic functionality like pairing the earbuds to a device is a nightmare. They disconnect randomly, refuse to reconnect unless reset, and occasionally pair to the wrong device altogether (yes, my smart fridge now has audio privileges). For a product that claims to prioritize performance, the sheer lack of reliability is inexcusable.4. Clarity? More Like ôMuddy MessöIf you value clarity in your audio experience, steer clear. The sound profile is so unbalanced itĺs comical. Bass is overblown to the point of distortion, mids are nonexistent, and highs are shrill enough to shatter glass. Listening to music feels like being trapped in a nightclub with blown-out speakersŚexcept even nightclubs have better sound quality.For podcasts or audiobooks, the lack of clarity turns every narrator into a garbled mess. Voices blend into the background noise, and subtle details are drowned out by the earbudsĺ own hissing. Even at maximum volume, dialogue feels distant, as if the speaker is standing in another room. The sound quality isnĺt just badŚitĺs offensive.5. Performance: A Race to the BottomThe performance of these earbuds is a slap in the face to anyone whoĺs ever used a functional pair. The battery life, advertised as ôup to 8 hours,ö barely lasts two hours at 50% volume. And charging them? The case takes longer to power up than a medieval siege engine. Even the earbuds functionality during calls is a disaster. Every conversation becomes a game of ôGuess What I Said,ö with the other person constantly asking you to repeat yourself.Under stressŚlike multitasking between apps or running background downloadsŚthe performance nosedives further. Audio stutters, the mic cuts out, and the earbuds emit a high-pitched whine that could double as a dog whistle. For a product that claims to prioritize performance, itĺs clear no one tested these under real-world conditions.6. Earbuds Functionality: A Comedy of ErrorsThe earbuds functionality extends beyond poor sound and controls. The ergonomic design is a joke. They donĺt fit securely in any ear shape, requiring constant adjustments mid-use. During a jog, one earbud flew out and nearly became roadkill. The ôsweat-resistantö coating? A myth. After one gym session, the left earbud shorted out, emitting a smell akin to burnt plastic.Even simple tasks, like skipping a track or answering a call, require Herculean effort. The touch-sensitive areas are tiny and poorly mapped, leading to accidental hangs-ups or unintended playlist shuffles. For earbuds functionality thatĺs supposed to simplify your life, these add nothing but frustration.7. Sound Quality: An Assault on the SensesThe sound quality is where these earbuds truly cement their status as the worst on the market. The audio is flat, tinny, and devoid of depthŚlike listening to music through a cardboard tube. Genres that rely on nuance, like classical or jazz, are rendered unrecognizable. Rock and pop tracks lose their energy, with guitars sounding like kazoos and vocals like autotuned robots.Even for non-audiophiles, the sound quality is unbearable. Watching movies? Dialogue is drowned out by inconsistent volume levels, and action scenes lack punch. The lack of clarity and dynamic range makes every audio experience feel like a punishment.8. Build Quality: Cheap, Flimsy, and Downright DangerousThe build quality is a testament to corner-cutting. The plastic feels like it was salvaged from a dollar-store toy, with seams that creak under minimal pressure. The charging caseĺs lid wobbles like a loose tooth, and the earbuds themselves are so fragile, Iĺm afraid to breathe on them too hard.Within a week, the right earbudĺs mesh grill fell off, exposing the speaker to dust and debris. The charging pins corroded after minimal use, and the caseĺs hinge now emits a death rattle every time itĺs opened. For a product that costs more than a meal for two, the build quality is unforgivable.Conclusion: A Perfect Storm of FailureIn summary, these earbuds fail at every metric: mic functionality, gaming purposes, functionality, clarity, performance, earbuds functionality, sound quality, and build quality. Theyĺre poorly designed, shoddily constructed, and utterly unfit for any use case. The sound quality is grating, the mic functionality is a joke, and the build quality is a safety hazard.If you value your time, money, and sanity, avoid these earbuds at all costs. For gaming purposes, communication, or even passive listening, there are countless better optionsŚincluding earbuds half the price. These arenĺt just bad; theyĺre an insult to the very idea of technology.Final Verdict: 0/10. The only thing these earbuds excel at is making you regret your purchase.Read more

The new pair had the same sound quality issues as the original one. Review Title: Bought it for its battery back-up but got more
Review Description: Let me start with the elephant in the room claim - 24 hours battery back up from buds alone. I received this TWS around 6:45 in the evening, took about a couple  minutes to check the box contents and make sure the TWS was in perfect condition. Paired them with my phone only to find out the earbuds had 100% charge, so I started using them. I initiated my "testing" at 7 p.m. with The Jurassic Park (1993) at about 80% volume. 2 hours later, the buds had ~93% charge left. I said, "Challenge accepted!", and then watched the sequel, The Lost World (1997). Another 2 hours and some odd minutes later, the time was 11:10 p.m., the left bud showed 86% battery and the right was at 85%. At this point, I could see myself losing the battle, but I didn't wanna give up. I thought to myself, "Maybe if I tried harder, I'd win." But time, as always, wasn't on my side. It was 11:20 p.m., the silence of the night could be heard loudly and the fatigue of the day was catching up to me, when suddenly, an idea popped into my head. I rushed over to YouTube, searched for a 10 hour heavy metal music video, hit the play button, increased the earbuds volume to 100%, and then put both my phone and the buds on the bedside table grinning to myself thinking, "Yeah, survive that.", and called it a night. The morning came and as my vision cleared up to show 7:20 a.m. on the clock, I reached out to my phone, assuredly and confidently convinced, I had won the war.I didn't. The right bud was at 41% and the left at 45%. At this juncture, I gladly accepted my defeat and decided to write this story, a story of a brave TWS, a story that will never be in any history books, a story where the eternal TWS won against its sadistic owner.As for the rest, I will keep it brief;In-ear Detection: Works very well, no complaints.Sound Quality: Signature mode is too bass-y, not for me, for sure. Balance mode does the job and it does it well.Call quality: THE BEST. Tested against Boat Rockers 255 Neo, Boult Audio X50, Dizo GoPods Neo, Realme T100.Build Quality: Buds good. Case lid, when closed, has a slight wobble to it. Everything else is upto the mark.One final remark would be in-ear fitting, and thus, noise isolation. I find these buds to provide noise isolation as good as Dizo Gopods Neo with its 25Db ANC mode on. It almost feels like they were custom made for my ears. But do keep in mind that this can and will differ from person to person.Thank you for your time and I hope I didn't waste it.Read more

They disconnect randomly, refuse to reconnect unless reset, and occasionally pair to the wrong device altogether (yes, my smart fridge now has audio privileges). For a product that claims to prioritize performance, the sheer lack of reliability is inexcusable.4. Clarity? More Like ôMuddy MessöIf you value clarity in your audio experience, steer clear. The sound profile is so unbalanced itĺs comical. Bass is overblown to the point of distortion, mids are nonexistent, and highs are shrill enough to shatter glass. Listening to music feels like being trapped in a nightclub with blown-out speakersŚexcept even nightclubs have better sound quality.For podcasts or audiobooks, the lack of clarity turns every narrator into a garbled mess. Voices blend into the background noise, and subtle details are drowned out by the earbudsĺ own hissing. Even at maximum volume, dialogue feels distant, as if the speaker is standing in another room. The sound quality isnĺt just badŚitĺs offensive.5. Performance: A Race to the BottomThe performance of these earbuds is a slap in the face to anyone whoĺs ever used a functional pair. The battery life, advertised as ôup to 8 hours,ö barely lasts two hours at 50% volume. And charging them? The case takes longer to power up than a medieval siege engine. Even the earbuds functionality during calls is a disaster. Every conversation becomes a game of ôGuess What I Said,ö with the other person constantly asking you to repeat yourself.Under stressŚlike multitasking between apps or running background downloadsŚthe performance nosedives further. Audio stutters, the mic cuts out, and the earbuds emit a high-pitched whine that could double as a dog whistle. For a product that claims to prioritize performance, itĺs clear no one tested these under real-world conditions.6. Earbuds Functionality: A Comedy of ErrorsThe earbuds functionality extends beyond poor sound and controls. The ergonomic design is a joke. They donĺt fit securely in any ear shape, requiring constant adjustments mid-use. During a jog, one earbud flew out and nearly became roadkill. The ôsweat-resistantö coating? A myth. After one gym session, the left earbud shorted out, emitting a smell akin to burnt plastic.Even simple tasks, like skipping a track or answering a call, require Herculean effort. The touch-sensitive areas are tiny and poorly mapped, leading to accidental hangs-ups or unintended playlist shuffles. For earbuds functionality thatĺs supposed to simplify your life, these add nothing but frustration.7. Sound Quality: An Assault on the SensesThe sound quality is where these earbuds truly cement their status as the worst on the market. The audio is flat, tinny, and devoid of depthŚlike listening to music through a cardboard tube. Genres that rely on nuance, like classical or jazz, are rendered unrecognizable. Rock and pop tracks lose their energy, with guitars sounding like kazoos and vocals like autotuned robots.Even for non-audiophiles, the sound quality is unbearable. Watching movies? Dialogue is drowned out by inconsistent volume levels, and action scenes lack punch. The lack of clarity and dynamic range makes every audio experience feel like a punishment.8. Build Quality: Cheap, Flimsy, and Downright DangerousThe build quality is a testament to corner-cutting. The plastic feels like it was salvaged from a dollar-store toy, with seams that creak under minimal pressure. The charging caseĺs lid wobbles like a loose tooth, and the earbuds themselves are so fragile, Iĺm afraid to breathe on them too hard.Within a week, the right earbudĺs mesh grill fell off, exposing the speaker to dust and debris. The charging pins corroded after minimal use, and the caseĺs hinge now emits a death rattle every time itĺs opened. For a product that costs more than a meal for two, the build quality is unforgivable.Conclusion: A Perfect Storm of FailureIn summary, these earbuds fail at every metric: mic functionality, gaming purposes, functionality, clarity, performance, earbuds functionality, sound quality, and build quality. Theyĺre poorly designed, shoddily constructed, and utterly unfit for any use case. The sound quality is grating, the mic functionality is a joke, and the build quality is a safety hazard.If you value your time, money, and sanity, avoid these earbuds at all costs. For gaming purposes, communication, or even passive listening, there are countless better optionsŚincluding earbuds half the price. These arenĺt just bad; theyĺre an insult to the very idea of technology.Final Verdict: 0/10. The only thing these earbuds excel at is making you regret your purchase.Read more

Review Title: AAn Audiophileĺs Take: Surprisingly good, but don't expect 7Hz Zero 2 performance
Review Description: The Reference Point (My Daily Driver):I want to be completely honest about my sound standards. My daily drivers are the 7Hz Salnotes Zero 2 wired IEMs. If you own those, you know exactly what "top-notch" clarity and "pure bass" feel likeŚwhere you can literally feel the sub-bass texture without any distortion.?The Comparison (Realme vs. IEMs):I bought the Realme Buds T200x to see how they stack up against a proper wired setup.?The Bass: The Realme T200x is good. For a wireless budget earbud, the bass is punchy and enjoyable. However, it is not on the same level as the 7Hz. It gets "close" to that signature sound, but it lacks that deep, "pure bass" feeling you get from the Zero 2s. Itĺs smooth, but not as immersive.?The Clarity: This is where the difference is most obvious. The 7Hz has crystal clear vocals. The Realme T200x vocal clarity is decent, but slightly recessed in comparison.?The Reality: A normal user won't notice these details and will love the sound. But if you are coming from IEMs, you will notice the difference.?Design & Comfort:As you can see in my photos, the matte finish and design are excellent. They definitely feel more premium than the price suggests, and the case is compact and pocketable.?Verdict:Don't buy these expecting them to replace your 7Hz Zero 2sŚwired audio is still king. But if you want a wireless pair that gets surprisingly close to that sound signature without the wires, these are a solid backup.?Pros:?Premium matte design.?Good bass for a wireless TWS.?No distortion at normal volumes.?Cons:?Vocal clarity isn't as sharp as wired IEMs.?Bass is good, but lacks the "pure" texture of high-end gear.Read more

Review Title: Bought it for its battery back-up but got more
Review Description: Let me start with the elephant in the room claim - 24 hours battery back up from buds alone. I received this TWS around 6:45 in the evening, took about a couple  minutes to check the box contents and make sure the TWS was in perfect condition. Paired them with my phone only to find out the earbuds had 100% charge, so I started using them. I initiated my "testing" at 7 p.m. with The Jurassic Park (1993) at about 80% volume. 2 hours later, the buds had ~93% charge left. I said, "Challenge accepted!", and then watched the sequel, The Lost World (1997). Another 2 hours and some odd minutes later, the time was 11:10 p.m., the left bud showed 86% battery and the right was at 85%. At this point, I could see myself losing the battle, but I didn't wanna give up. I thought to myself, "Maybe if I tried harder, I'd win." But time, as always, wasn't on my side. It was 11:20 p.m., the silence of the night could be heard loudly and the fatigue of the day was catching up to me, when suddenly, an idea popped into my head. I rushed over to YouTube, searched for a 10 hour heavy metal music video, hit the play button, increased the earbuds volume to 100%, and then put both my phone and the buds on the bedside table grinning to myself thinking, "Yeah, survive that.", and called it a night. The morning came and as my vision cleared up to show 7:20 a.m. on the clock, I reached out to my phone, assuredly and confidently convinced, I had won the war.I didn't. The right bud was at 41% and the left at 45%. At this juncture, I gladly accepted my defeat and decided to write this story, a story of a brave TWS, a story that will never be in any history books, a story where the eternal TWS won against its sadistic owner.As for the rest, I will keep it brief;In-ear Detection: Works very well, no complaints.Sound Quality: Signature mode is too bass-y, not for me, for sure. Balance mode does the job and it does it well.Call quality: THE BEST. Tested against Boat Rockers 255 Neo, Boult Audio X50, Dizo GoPods Neo, Realme T100.Build Quality: Buds good. Case lid, when closed, has a slight wobble to it. Everything else is upto the mark.One final remark would be in-ear fitting, and thus, noise isolation. I find these buds to provide noise isolation as good as Dizo Gopods Neo with its 25Db ANC mode on. It almost feels like they were custom made for my ears. But do keep in mind that this can and will differ from person to person.Thank you for your time and I hope I didn't waste it.Read more

Review Title: The Ultimate Disappointment: A Scathing Review of the Worst Earbuds Iĺve Ever Used
Review Description: The media could not be loaded.Let me preface this review by saying Iĺve tested countless earbuds over the years, ranging from budget-friendly options to premium audiophile gear. Never in my life have I encountered a product as fundamentally flawed, poorly designed, and outright infuriating as these earbuds. If youĺre considering purchasing these for gaming purposes, casual listening, or even as a backup pairŚSTOP. Save your money, your sanity, and your ears. From the abysmal mic functionality to the laughable sound quality, and the build quality that feels like it was engineered by someone whoĺs never held a pair of earbuds before, this product is a masterclass in failure. Letĺs break down why these earbuds are an insult to the very concept of functionality, clarity, and performance.1. Mic Functionality: A Joke for Gaming PurposesLetĺs start with the mic functionality, a feature thatĺs absolutely critical for gaming purposes or even casual Zoom calls. If youĺre buying earbuds to communicate with teammates, stream, or participate in online meetings, prepare to be humiliated. The microphone picks up every conceivable noise except your voice. Background chatter, the faint hum of your refrigerator, even your dog barking three rooms awayŚall of it is crystal clear. But your actual voice? Muffled, distorted, and buried under a layer of static that makes you sound like youĺre speaking through a tin can underwater.During a critical raid in my favorite MMO, my teammates repeatedly asked, ôAre you chewing gravel? Why does your mic sound like a broken walkie-talkie?ö When I switched to these earbudsĺ mic functionality, the response was unanimous: ôNever use these again.ö For gaming purposes, where clear communication is non-negotiable, this mic is a liability. Even after tweaking settings, updating drivers, and screaming into the microphone like a madman, the clarity never improved. Itĺs almost impressive how a company could engineer a microphone this bad.2. Gaming Purpose? More Like ôGame OveröSpeaking of gaming purposes, these earbuds fail on every front. Latency is so severe that actions in-gameŚlike gunfire or footstepsŚare delayed by a full second, making competitive play impossible. Imagine hearing an enemyĺs shotgun blast after youĺve already been respawned. The lack of clarity in spatial audio means you canĺt distinguish between sounds coming from your left or right, a death sentence in shooters or horror games.But the real kicker? The performance of these earbuds under load is laughable. After just 30 minutes of use, they overheat to the point of discomfort, and the audio begins to crackle like a campfire. For a product marketed toward gaming purposes, the complete disregard for basic functionality is staggering.3. Functionality: A Textbook Example of ôHow Not to Design EarbudsöThe functionality of these earbuds is so counterintuitive, it feels like a prank. The touch controls are hypersensitive yet unresponsiveŚa paradox that defies logic. Adjusting the volume? Prepare for a 50/50 chance of either muting the audio entirely or triggering your voice assistant. The companion app, which promises to ôenhance your experience,ö is a glitchy mess that crashes more often than a toddlerĺs toy car.Even basic functionality like pairing the earbuds to a device is a nightmare. They disconnect randomly, refuse to reconnect unless reset, and occasionally pair to the wrong device altogether (yes, my smart fridge now has audio privileges). For a product that claims to prioritize performance, the sheer lack of reliability is inexcusable.4. Clarity? More Like ôMuddy MessöIf you value clarity in your audio experience, steer clear. The sound profile is so unbalanced itĺs comical. Bass is overblown to the point of distortion, mids are nonexistent, and highs are shrill enough to shatter glass. Listening to music feels like being trapped in a nightclub with blown-out speakersŚexcept even nightclubs have better sound quality.For podcasts or audiobooks, the lack of clarity turns every narrator into a garbled mess. Voices blend into the background noise, and subtle details are drowned out by the earbudsĺ own hissing. Even at maximum volume, dialogue feels distant, as if the speaker is standing in another room. The sound quality isnĺt just badŚitĺs offensive.5. Performance: A Race to the BottomThe performance of these earbuds is a slap in the face to anyone whoĺs ever used a functional pair. The battery life, advertised as ôup to 8 hours,ö barely lasts two hours at 50% volume. And charging them? The case takes longer to power up than a medieval siege engine. Even the earbuds functionality during calls is a disaster. Every conversation becomes a game of ôGuess What I Said,ö with the other person constantly asking you to repeat yourself.Under stressŚlike multitasking between apps or running background downloadsŚthe performance nosedives further. Audio stutters, the mic cuts out, and the earbuds emit a high-pitched whine that could double as a dog whistle. For a product that claims to prioritize performance, itĺs clear no one tested these under real-world conditions.6. Earbuds Functionality: A Comedy of ErrorsThe earbuds functionality extends beyond poor sound and controls. The ergonomic design is a joke. They donĺt fit securely in any ear shape, requiring constant adjustments mid-use. During a jog, one earbud flew out and nearly became roadkill. The ôsweat-resistantö coating? A myth. After one gym session, the left earbud shorted out, emitting a smell akin to burnt plastic.Even simple tasks, like skipping a track or answering a call, require Herculean effort. The touch-sensitive areas are tiny and poorly mapped, leading to accidental hangs-ups or unintended playlist shuffles. For earbuds functionality thatĺs supposed to simplify your life, these add nothing but frustration.7. Sound Quality: An Assault on the SensesThe sound quality is where these earbuds truly cement their status as the worst on the market. The audio is flat, tinny, and devoid of depthŚlike listening to music through a cardboard tube. Genres that rely on nuance, like classical or jazz, are rendered unrecognizable. Rock and pop tracks lose their energy, with guitars sounding like kazoos and vocals like autotuned robots.Even for non-audiophiles, the sound quality is unbearable. Watching movies? Dialogue is drowned out by inconsistent volume levels, and action scenes lack punch. The lack of clarity and dynamic range makes every audio experience feel like a punishment.8. Build Quality: Cheap, Flimsy, and Downright DangerousThe build quality is a testament to corner-cutting. The plastic feels like it was salvaged from a dollar-store toy, with seams that creak under minimal pressure. The charging caseĺs lid wobbles like a loose tooth, and the earbuds themselves are so fragile, Iĺm afraid to breathe on them too hard.Within a week, the right earbudĺs mesh grill fell off, exposing the speaker to dust and debris. The charging pins corroded after minimal use, and the caseĺs hinge now emits a death rattle every time itĺs opened. For a product that costs more than a meal for two, the build quality is unforgivable.Conclusion: A Perfect Storm of FailureIn summary, these earbuds fail at every metric: mic functionality, gaming purposes, functionality, clarity, performance, earbuds functionality, sound quality, and build quality. Theyĺre poorly designed, shoddily constructed, and utterly unfit for any use case. The sound quality is grating, the mic functionality is a joke, and the build quality is a safety hazard.If you value your time, money, and sanity, avoid these earbuds at all costs. For gaming purposes, communication, or even passive listening, there are countless better optionsŚincluding earbuds half the price. These arenĺt just bad; theyĺre an insult to the very idea of technology.Final Verdict: 0/10. The only thing these earbuds excel at is making you regret your purchase.Read more

Review Title: Absolutely Blown Away by the boAt Airdopes 300 - My New Audio Holy Grail! (Gunmetal Black)
Review Description: Absolutely Blown Away by the boAt Airdopes 300 - My New Audio Holy Grail! (Gunmetal Black)Rating: ????? (5/5)Okay, I need to rave about these! After seeing the hype (and that 5K+ bought last month stat!), I grabbed the boAt Airdopes 300 in Gunmetal Black, and honestly? They've exceeded every expectation. If you're looking for premium features without the insane price tag, stop scrolling ľ these are it.Here's why I'm obsessed:1.  Cinematic Spatial Audio is REAL: This isn't just marketing fluff. Watching movies or binge-watching shows is a whole new experience. The sound genuinely feels like it's coming from around you ľ immersive, deep, and incredibly engaging. Music benefits massively too, feeling richer and more layered than any earbuds I've owned before.2.  Battery Life That Defies Logic: 50 HOURS?! This is the absolute game-changer. The earbuds themselves last ages (around 10 hours on a single charge for me), but the charging case just keeps going. I charged the case fully over a week ago, used the buds daily for hours (commutes, workouts, calls), and I still haven't seen a low battery warning. It's liberating not to constantly worry about charging. MASSIVE win.3.  Crystal Clear Calls with 4-Mic AI ENx: Taking calls on these is a dream. I've tested them on busy streets, windy balconies, and even in a slightly noisy cafe ľ the person on the other end consistently says I sound clear and focused. The AI noise cancellation for the mics really works wonders. No more "Can you repeat that?" frustrations.4.  Fast Charge is a Lifesaver: Forgot to charge? 10 minutes in the case genuinely gives you hours (around 2-2.5 in my experience) of playback. Super convenient when you're rushing out.5.  boAt App = Awesome Customization: The app is simple but powerful. I love tweaking the EQ presets (bass boost for workouts, podcast mode for clarity) and customizing the touch controls. Finding the earbuds via the app is also a handy feature.6.  Rock-Solid Connection & Low Latency: Bluetooth 5.3 is flawless. Pairing was instant, and the connection stays strong even when my phone is in another room. Gaming and watching videos is seamless ľ absolutely zero noticeable lag or audio-video sync issues.7.  Comfort & Build: The Gunmetal Black looks and feels super premium. They fit snugly and comfortably in my ears, even during longer sessions or workouts. The IPX4 rating gives me peace of mind against sweat and light rain.8.  Great Sound Signature: Bass is punchy and satisfying without being overwhelming, mids are clear for vocals, and highs are crisp. Perfectly tuned for everything from EDM to podcasts.Minor Nitpick (because nothing's perfect):The touch controls are sensitive (great!), but there's a slight learning curve to avoid accidental pauses/skips when adjusting them. You get used to it quickly though.Verdict:The boAt Airdopes 300 deliver phenomenal value. You get flagship-level features ľ incredible 50H battery life, truly immersive spatial audio, excellent call quality with AI ENx, fast charging, app support, low latency, and a sleek design ľ all at a very reasonable price point. The hype is real, and the 5K+ purchases last month make total sense.If you're looking for reliable, feature-packed, great-sounding TWS earbuds that won't die on you halfway through the day, look no further. The Airdopes 300 in Gunmetal Black are an easy 5-star recommendation! boAt has absolutely knocked it out of the park with these. Highly impressed!Read more

Review Title: Excellent, Worth The Money But Conditions Apply
Review Description: Long detailed review but it hope it offers some perspectiveEveryone's ear is different. There is no "best" earbuds. Whats best for me may not be best you. So first and foremost, decide what type of music you listen to and whats your purpose of buying a TWSMy review is based on comparison with Galaxy Buds Plus (2020 Flagship Buds from Samsung) which J have been using till date. So my expectation was set very high from the begining. So how does this stand in front of a flagship at 1/3rd of the cost ?1. Fit and Finish: Its about the same as Galaxy buds plus in terms of looks, size and fit (I use the largest eartips). So if you are happy with buds plus, you won't get any complains from with Buds Core2. In terms of features, you lose the flagship features in Buds Core. That means no wireless charging, no in ear detection for music. No even a charging cable ?? But thats about it. Instead, you get ANC, better mics, better ambient mode, better battery.3. Coming  to the most important part, the sound quality: Here I was surprised. The sound quality is almost same as the flagship buds plus. But with buds core, you get much better bass. You will love it. It doesn't overpower other instruments, vocals but you can feel the punchiness. But if you are someone who prioritizes bass above everything else, this isn't for you.A small caveat: I have been using Samsung S series since 2020. Samsung has its own codec (SSC). So to make use of the full capability of any Samsung TWS. you need a Samsung device. It will work on other phones but may not give the best experience. But if you have a Samsung phone, no meed to look anywhere else, except if you need bass only.Also try to stay away from marketing terms like Hi-Res support, LHDC, etc, especially in budget TWS or headphones. They are just codec support, which means your TWS can play Hi-Res audio files. To get real Hi-Res audio experience, you need bigger physical drivers (40mm or more), powerful DSPs, much bigger battery. Thats why when you turn on the Hi-Res codecs, battery will drain like money from your wallet after 1st day of the month, even in the XMs, QCs, Momentums.Its impossible for TWS to have that type of capability. At best, due to hi-res codec support, the DSP may upscale normal songs by, maybe 5-10%, but that will come at a cost of battery and little to no listening difference unless you are a big audiophile. Think from a practical point of view, hi-res music are always behind a paywall. How many people who are buying budget TWS/headphones will be going to pay for apple music or spotify premium or amazon HD music ?That bring said, neither buds plus has any hi res, nor the buds core will give it. I also have a Sony WH CH720N which is a full fledged ANC headphone and it doesn't have any hi res either. Neither the Sony ULT 900 which costs above 10k come with hi res certification.My take: If you like the sound, go buy it. If it comes with hi res codec support, take it as a bonus feature but don't make it a make a break decision. Support for hi res means nothing if the driver tunning itself is bad.4. Calling: Calling is better on buds core than buds plus because it has 3 mics per bud i.e. 6 mics. So no complains there.5. ANC: This is the most misunderstood feature and most people fall for gimmicks. Don't fall terms like 50db, 55db, 45db ANC. These numbers mean nothing. ANC is an algorithm. So the only metric that matters is consistency and how smart it is. An inconsistent ANC may work indoors but fail in traffic. Or work well in traffic but fail in a coffee shop. Also ANC isn't there to give you a feeling of empty space (thats what earplugs are for), its there so that the other party can listen to your voice clearly. Thats when the smartness of ANC becomes clear. A badly implemented ANC would simply cut all the sound that fall below certain db. This doesn't matter during music listening but becomes a horrible experience in calls because it will cut your voice too. Hence the statement "whats your purpose of buying a TWS" in the begining.Long story short, if your usage is calls as well as music and your environment is mostky indoors and some traffic (not the traffics in which everyone keeps honking), buds core ANC is more than enough to cut down approx 80% or more. But lets says your friend is talking near you while you are on phone, you and the other party may hear it. Thats not bad ANC, thats a compromise the ANC algorithm is making to preserve your voice clarity. Now other brands ANC algorithm may decide to cut down the voice. So you and other party will feel quiter but your voice will sound muffled to the other party. Now the Sony XMs, Bose QCs work better in these scenarios but they also cost 15x more. Thats what the extra money is for.Read more

Review Title: OnePlus Nord Buds 3: Rapid Failure, Questionable Durability & Disappointing Service
Review Description: The media could not be loaded.I purchased the OnePlus Nord Buds 3 less than three months ago, hoping for a reliable audio companion for daily use. As someone who uses earphones more than the average person, durability and a reasonable level of resistance are important factors for me. Size and fit were fine, I was pleased with it's performance until the issues started.Usage & Expectations:My usage pattern involves frequent listening, primarily indoors in air-conditioned environments. While I do sweat more than average, I specifically avoid using earphones during workouts. My exposure to sweat typically happens during commutes or when stepping outside briefly. Given the IP55 rating, which suggests protection against dust ingress and low-pressure water jets, I reasonably expected the Nord Buds 3 to handle this level of sweat ľ equivalent to what a regular person might encounter. I never exposed them to rain or dropped them in water.The Failure Point - After Just 10 Weeks:Unfortunately, my experience fell drastically short. In just around 10 weeks (less than 3 months), I started experiencing significant connection issues. Upon inspection, I noticed corrosion or damage on the charging contact points. Significantly, all four contact points on the buds were affected simultaneously.The connection problems manifested in frustrating ways:Even after placing the buds back into the charging case, my phone would often still show them as connected and in use, draining their battery unnecessarily.Conversely, sometimes when taking the buds out of the case, they wouldn't connect or function at all, acting as if they were still stored inside.This erratic behavior points directly to the damaged contact points failing to correctly signal whether the buds were docked or undocked. While sweat is corrosive, the simultaneous failure of all four points in such a short time raises questions about whether this was solely due to sweat ingress or potentially indicative of a manufacturing defect or design flaw compromising the seals or contact materials. The IP55 rating certainly led me to believe they should have withstood this level of exposure.Horrible Service Experience:Seeking a solution under warranty, I visited the OnePlus service center. I clearly pointed out the damage to the contact points and described the specific connection issues. Despite this, I was made to wait for approximately 45 minutes, only to be told that the warranty claim was denied due to "liquid damage," citing the shorted contact points.This response was incredibly frustrating. The service felt dismissive, with no real consideration given to the rapid failure time, the specific symptoms linked directly to contact failure, the simultaneous failure of all contacts, or the potential discrepancy between the advertised IP rating and real-world performance.Warranty Policy vs. Expectation:It was only after this denial that the specifics of the warranty became starkly clear. I understand now that OnePlus's official warranty policy may explicitly exclude any form of liquid damage, including sweat. However, even knowing the policy, the overall experience remains deeply unsatisfactory. For a product rated IP55 to fail from moderate, non-exercise sweat exposure in such a short timeframe is concerning. I expected a better experience ľ both from the product lasting significantly longer than 10 weeks and from a customer service interaction that felt less like an immediate dismissal.Conclusion:My experience with the OnePlus Nord Buds 3 has been extremely disappointing. The earphones failed completely in about 10 weeks due to apparent sweat damage causing critical contact point failure, despite an IP55 rating and careful, non-exercise use. The simultaneous failure of all contact points is suspicious. While OnePlus may not cover liquid damage under warranty according to their policy, the rapid product failure combined with a dismissive and unhelpful service experience leaves a very poor impression.Based on this, I cannot recommend the OnePlus Nord Buds 3, especially to individuals who might expose them to even moderate sweat. The practical durability seems questionable, and the after-sales support was lacking. Potential buyers should be wary. I am now forced to find replacement earphones far sooner than acceptable.Read more

Review Title: Bought it for its battery back-up but got more
Review Description: Let me start with the elephant in the room claim - 24 hours battery back up from buds alone. I received this TWS around 6:45 in the evening, took about a couple  minutes to check the box contents and make sure the TWS was in perfect condition. Paired them with my phone only to find out the earbuds had 100% charge, so I started using them. I initiated my "testing" at 7 p.m. with The Jurassic Park (1993) at about 80% volume. 2 hours later, the buds had ~93% charge left. I said, "Challenge accepted!", and then watched the sequel, The Lost World (1997). Another 2 hours and some odd minutes later, the time was 11:10 p.m., the left bud showed 86% battery and the right was at 85%. At this point, I could see myself losing the battle, but I didn't wanna give up. I thought to myself, "Maybe if I tried harder, I'd win." But time, as always, wasn't on my side. It was 11:20 p.m., the silence of the night could be heard loudly and the fatigue of the day was catching up to me, when suddenly, an idea popped into my head. I rushed over to YouTube, searched for a 10 hour heavy metal music video, hit the play button, increased the earbuds volume to 100%, and then put both my phone and the buds on the bedside table grinning to myself thinking, "Yeah, survive that.", and called it a night. The morning came and as my vision cleared up to show 7:20 a.m. on the clock, I reached out to my phone, assuredly and confidently convinced, I had won the war.I didn't. The right bud was at 41% and the left at 45%. At this juncture, I gladly accepted my defeat and decided to write this story, a story of a brave TWS, a story that will never be in any history books, a story where the eternal TWS won against its sadistic owner.As for the rest, I will keep it brief;In-ear Detection: Works very well, no complaints.Sound Quality: Signature mode is too bass-y, not for me, for sure. Balance mode does the job and it does it well.Call quality: THE BEST. Tested against Boat Rockers 255 Neo, Boult Audio X50, Dizo GoPods Neo, Realme T100.Build Quality: Buds good. Case lid, when closed, has a slight wobble to it. Everything else is upto the mark.One final remark would be in-ear fitting, and thus, noise isolation. I find these buds to provide noise isolation as good as Dizo Gopods Neo with its 25Db ANC mode on. It almost feels like they were custom made for my ears. But do keep in mind that this can and will differ from person to person.Thank you for your time and I hope I didn't waste it.Read more

Review Title: The Ultimate Disappointment: A Scathing Review of the Worst Earbuds Iĺve Ever Used
Review Description: The media could not be loaded.Let me preface this review by saying Iĺve tested countless earbuds over the years, ranging from budget-friendly options to premium audiophile gear. Never in my life have I encountered a product as fundamentally flawed, poorly designed, and outright infuriating as these earbuds. If youĺre considering purchasing these for gaming purposes, casual listening, or even as a backup pairŚSTOP. Save your money, your sanity, and your ears. From the abysmal mic functionality to the laughable sound quality, and the build quality that feels like it was engineered by someone whoĺs never held a pair of earbuds before, this product is a masterclass in failure. Letĺs break down why these earbuds are an insult to the very concept of functionality, clarity, and performance.1. Mic Functionality: A Joke for Gaming PurposesLetĺs start with the mic functionality, a feature thatĺs absolutely critical for gaming purposes or even casual Zoom calls. If youĺre buying earbuds to communicate with teammates, stream, or participate in online meetings, prepare to be humiliated. The microphone picks up every conceivable noise except your voice. Background chatter, the faint hum of your refrigerator, even your dog barking three rooms awayŚall of it is crystal clear. But your actual voice? Muffled, distorted, and buried under a layer of static that makes you sound like youĺre speaking through a tin can underwater.During a critical raid in my favorite MMO, my teammates repeatedly asked, ôAre you chewing gravel? Why does your mic sound like a broken walkie-talkie?ö When I switched to these earbudsĺ mic functionality, the response was unanimous: ôNever use these again.ö For gaming purposes, where clear communication is non-negotiable, this mic is a liability. Even after tweaking settings, updating drivers, and screaming into the microphone like a madman, the clarity never improved. Itĺs almost impressive how a company could engineer a microphone this bad.2. Gaming Purpose? More Like ôGame OveröSpeaking of gaming purposes, these earbuds fail on every front. Latency is so severe that actions in-gameŚlike gunfire or footstepsŚare delayed by a full second, making competitive play impossible. Imagine hearing an enemyĺs shotgun blast after youĺve already been respawned. The lack of clarity in spatial audio means you canĺt distinguish between sounds coming from your left or right, a death sentence in shooters or horror games.But the real kicker? The performance of these earbuds under load is laughable. After just 30 minutes of use, they overheat to the point of discomfort, and the audio begins to crackle like a campfire. For a product marketed toward gaming purposes, the complete disregard for basic functionality is staggering.3. Functionality: A Textbook Example of ôHow Not to Design EarbudsöThe functionality of these earbuds is so counterintuitive, it feels like a prank. The touch controls are hypersensitive yet unresponsiveŚa paradox that defies logic. Adjusting the volume? Prepare for a 50/50 chance of either muting the audio entirely or triggering your voice assistant. The companion app, which promises to ôenhance your experience,ö is a glitchy mess that crashes more often than a toddlerĺs toy car.Even basic functionality like pairing the earbuds to a device is a nightmare. They disconnect randomly, refuse to reconnect unless reset, and occasionally pair to the wrong device altogether (yes, my smart fridge now has audio privileges). For a product that claims to prioritize performance, the sheer lack of reliability is inexcusable.4. Clarity? More Like ôMuddy MessöIf you value clarity in your audio experience, steer clear. The sound profile is so unbalanced itĺs comical. Bass is overblown to the point of distortion, mids are nonexistent, and highs are shrill enough to shatter glass. Listening to music feels like being trapped in a nightclub with blown-out speakersŚexcept even nightclubs have better sound quality.For podcasts or audiobooks, the lack of clarity turns every narrator into a garbled mess. Voices blend into the background noise, and subtle details are drowned out by the earbudsĺ own hissing. Even at maximum volume, dialogue feels distant, as if the speaker is standing in another room. The sound quality isnĺt just badŚitĺs offensive.5. Performance: A Race to the BottomThe performance of these earbuds is a slap in the face to anyone whoĺs ever used a functional pair. The battery life, advertised as ôup to 8 hours,ö barely lasts two hours at 50% volume. And charging them? The case takes longer to power up than a medieval siege engine. Even the earbuds functionality during calls is a disaster. Every conversation becomes a game of ôGuess What I Said,ö with the other person constantly asking you to repeat yourself.Under stressŚlike multitasking between apps or running background downloadsŚthe performance nosedives further. Audio stutters, the mic cuts out, and the earbuds emit a high-pitched whine that could double as a dog whistle. For a product that claims to prioritize performance, itĺs clear no one tested these under real-world conditions.6. Earbuds Functionality: A Comedy of ErrorsThe earbuds functionality extends beyond poor sound and controls. The ergonomic design is a joke. They donĺt fit securely in any ear shape, requiring constant adjustments mid-use. During a jog, one earbud flew out and nearly became roadkill. The ôsweat-resistantö coating? A myth. After one gym session, the left earbud shorted out, emitting a smell akin to burnt plastic.Even simple tasks, like skipping a track or answering a call, require Herculean effort. The touch-sensitive areas are tiny and poorly mapped, leading to accidental hangs-ups or unintended playlist shuffles. For earbuds functionality thatĺs supposed to simplify your life, these add nothing but frustration.7. Sound Quality: An Assault on the SensesThe sound quality is where these earbuds truly cement their status as the worst on the market. The audio is flat, tinny, and devoid of depthŚlike listening to music through a cardboard tube. Genres that rely on nuance, like classical or jazz, are rendered unrecognizable. Rock and pop tracks lose their energy, with guitars sounding like kazoos and vocals like autotuned robots.Even for non-audiophiles, the sound quality is unbearable. Watching movies? Dialogue is drowned out by inconsistent volume levels, and action scenes lack punch. The lack of clarity and dynamic range makes every audio experience feel like a punishment.8. Build Quality: Cheap, Flimsy, and Downright DangerousThe build quality is a testament to corner-cutting. The plastic feels like it was salvaged from a dollar-store toy, with seams that creak under minimal pressure. The charging caseĺs lid wobbles like a loose tooth, and the earbuds themselves are so fragile, Iĺm afraid to breathe on them too hard.Within a week, the right earbudĺs mesh grill fell off, exposing the speaker to dust and debris. The charging pins corroded after minimal use, and the caseĺs hinge now emits a death rattle every time itĺs opened. For a product that costs more than a meal for two, the build quality is unforgivable.Conclusion: A Perfect Storm of FailureIn summary, these earbuds fail at every metric: mic functionality, gaming purposes, functionality, clarity, performance, earbuds functionality, sound quality, and build quality. Theyĺre poorly designed, shoddily constructed, and utterly unfit for any use case. The sound quality is grating, the mic functionality is a joke, and the build quality is a safety hazard.If you value your time, money, and sanity, avoid these earbuds at all costs. For gaming purposes, communication, or even passive listening, there are countless better optionsŚincluding earbuds half the price. These arenĺt just bad; theyĺre an insult to the very idea of technology.Final Verdict: 0/10. The only thing these earbuds excel at is making you regret your purchase.Read more

Review Title: Excellent, Worth The Money But Conditions Apply
Review Description: Long detailed review but it hope it offers some perspectiveEveryone's ear is different. There is no "best" earbuds. Whats best for me may not be best you. So first and foremost, decide what type of music you listen to and whats your purpose of buying a TWSMy review is based on comparison with Galaxy Buds Plus (2020 Flagship Buds from Samsung) which J have been using till date. So my expectation was set very high from the begining. So how does this stand in front of a flagship at 1/3rd of the cost ?1. Fit and Finish: Its about the same as Galaxy buds plus in terms of looks, size and fit (I use the largest eartips). So if you are happy with buds plus, you won't get any complains from with Buds Core2. In terms of features, you lose the flagship features in Buds Core. That means no wireless charging, no in ear detection for music. No even a charging cable ?? But thats about it. Instead, you get ANC, better mics, better ambient mode, better battery.3. Coming  to the most important part, the sound quality: Here I was surprised. The sound quality is almost same as the flagship buds plus. But with buds core, you get much better bass. You will love it. It doesn't overpower other instruments, vocals but you can feel the punchiness. But if you are someone who prioritizes bass above everything else, this isn't for you.A small caveat: I have been using Samsung S series since 2020. Samsung has its own codec (SSC). So to make use of the full capability of any Samsung TWS. you need a Samsung device. It will work on other phones but may not give the best experience. But if you have a Samsung phone, no meed to look anywhere else, except if you need bass only.Also try to stay away from marketing terms like Hi-Res support, LHDC, etc, especially in budget TWS or headphones. They are just codec support, which means your TWS can play Hi-Res audio files. To get real Hi-Res audio experience, you need bigger physical drivers (40mm or more), powerful DSPs, much bigger battery. Thats why when you turn on the Hi-Res codecs, battery will drain like money from your wallet after 1st day of the month, even in the XMs, QCs, Momentums.Its impossible for TWS to have that type of capability. At best, due to hi-res codec support, the DSP may upscale normal songs by, maybe 5-10%, but that will come at a cost of battery and little to no listening difference unless you are a big audiophile. Think from a practical point of view, hi-res music are always behind a paywall. How many people who are buying budget TWS/headphones will be going to pay for apple music or spotify premium or amazon HD music ?That bring said, neither buds plus has any hi res, nor the buds core will give it. I also have a Sony WH CH720N which is a full fledged ANC headphone and it doesn't have any hi res either. Neither the Sony ULT 900 which costs above 10k come with hi res certification.My take: If you like the sound, go buy it. If it comes with hi res codec support, take it as a bonus feature but don't make it a make a break decision. Support for hi res means nothing if the driver tunning itself is bad.4. Calling: Calling is better on buds core than buds plus because it has 3 mics per bud i.e. 6 mics. So no complains there.5. ANC: This is the most misunderstood feature and most people fall for gimmicks. Don't fall terms like 50db, 55db, 45db ANC. These numbers mean nothing. ANC is an algorithm. So the only metric that matters is consistency and how smart it is. An inconsistent ANC may work indoors but fail in traffic. Or work well in traffic but fail in a coffee shop. Also ANC isn't there to give you a feeling of empty space (thats what earplugs are for), its there so that the other party can listen to your voice clearly. Thats when the smartness of ANC becomes clear. A badly implemented ANC would simply cut all the sound that fall below certain db. This doesn't matter during music listening but becomes a horrible experience in calls because it will cut your voice too. Hence the statement "whats your purpose of buying a TWS" in the begining.Long story short, if your usage is calls as well as music and your environment is mostky indoors and some traffic (not the traffics in which everyone keeps honking), buds core ANC is more than enough to cut down approx 80% or more. But lets says your friend is talking near you while you are on phone, you and the other party may hear it. Thats not bad ANC, thats a compromise the ANC algorithm is making to preserve your voice clarity. Now other brands ANC algorithm may decide to cut down the voice. So you and other party will feel quiter but your voice will sound muffled to the other party. Now the Sony XMs, Bose QCs work better in these scenarios but they also cost 15x more. Thats what the extra money is for.Read more

Review Title: Great Sound, Comfortable Fit, and Seamless Samsung Integration
Review Description: Samsung Galaxy Buds 4 Pro ľ Great Upgrade from Oppo Enco X2 (Especially for Samsung Users)I upgraded from the Oppo Enco X2, which were honestly fantastic and very underrated earbuds. Because of that, my expectations were pretty high. After using the Samsung Buds 4 Pro for a while, I can confidently say they feel like a solid upgrade for my use.First Impressions & DesignI really like the design and packaging. I chose the black color because I didnĺt want the AirPods-like look that the metallic finish gives.I use a Samsung S23, and the pairing experience was completely seamless. No additional app installation was needed - all the earbud controls and settings are integrated directly into the phone settings. The ecosystem integration is definitely a big advantage if you use Samsung devices.Comfort & FitThe fit is excellent. Iĺve worn them for long periods during the day and they remain comfortable with no ear fatigue. Theyĺre lightweight and stay secure in the ears.Sound QualityThis is where the earbuds really shine.Out of the box the sound is good, but it becomes much better after tuning the EQ. I did the ear fitting test and experimented with the equalizer settings.I found Dynamic EQ or a custom EQ to work best.What stood out:- Clear instrument separation- Punchy and controlled bass- Customizable sound depending on your tasteIĺve been listening to a lot of techno lately, and these earbuds handle it really well.Iĺm also using UHQ codec support, which likely helps extract the best sound quality.Compared to my Enco X2:- Bass feels stronger- Instrument separation is clearer- Tonal balance can be easily adjusted with EQANC & Ambient ModeThe ANC is good - better than the Enco X2 in my experience, though maybe slightly behind AirPods.Ambient mode is also improved and sounds fairly natural. That said, these arenĺt features I personally rely on a lot, so I didnĺt test them extensively.Controls & Smart FeaturesOne feature I unexpectedly love is the voice commands and gesture controls.Being able to say things like:- ôPlay musicö- ôStop musicö- ôVolume up / downöůand have the earbuds respond instantly is incredibly convenient.I also set up Gemini, which works seamlessly through the earbuds.Call QualityCall quality has been surprisingly good. Friends told me I sounded pretty normal during calls.So far Iĺve only tested in quiet environments, but it already seems better than the Enco X2, which used to pick up a lot of surrounding noise.Other FeaturesThereĺs also 360 audio, though personally I wasnĺt a big fan when I tried it. It might work better for certain content like movies.The settings themselves arenĺt overloaded with marketing terms, but there are enough customization options to make the earbuds fun to tweak.Pros? Excellent sound quality with EQ tuning? Comfortable and lightweight for long use? Seamless integration with Samsung phones? Useful voice commands and gesture controls? Good call qualityCons? Premium pricing? ANC may not be the absolute best compared to some competitors? 360 audio didnĺt impress me muchFinal VerdictFor me, this is definitely an upgrade from the Oppo Enco X2.If youĺre already in the Samsung ecosystem, these are an easy recommendation. The integration alone makes the experience much smoother.I was debating between Sony, Bose, and Samsung, but so far I have no regrets choosing Samsung.Read more

Review Title: Bought it for its battery back-up but got more
Review Description: Let me start with the elephant in the room claim - 24 hours battery back up from buds alone. I received this TWS around 6:45 in the evening, took about a couple  minutes to check the box contents and make sure the TWS was in perfect condition. Paired them with my phone only to find out the earbuds had 100% charge, so I started using them. I initiated my "testing" at 7 p.m. with The Jurassic Park (1993) at about 80% volume. 2 hours later, the buds had ~93% charge left. I said, "Challenge accepted!", and then watched the sequel, The Lost World (1997). Another 2 hours and some odd minutes later, the time was 11:10 p.m., the left bud showed 86% battery and the right was at 85%. At this point, I could see myself losing the battle, but I didn't wanna give up. I thought to myself, "Maybe if I tried harder, I'd win." But time, as always, wasn't on my side. It was 11:20 p.m., the silence of the night could be heard loudly and the fatigue of the day was catching up to me, when suddenly, an idea popped into my head. I rushed over to YouTube, searched for a 10 hour heavy metal music video, hit the play button, increased the earbuds volume to 100%, and then put both my phone and the buds on the bedside table grinning to myself thinking, "Yeah, survive that.", and called it a night. The morning came and as my vision cleared up to show 7:20 a.m. on the clock, I reached out to my phone, assuredly and confidently convinced, I had won the war.I didn't. The right bud was at 41% and the left at 45%. At this juncture, I gladly accepted my defeat and decided to write this story, a story of a brave TWS, a story that will never be in any history books, a story where the eternal TWS won against its sadistic owner.As for the rest, I will keep it brief;In-ear Detection: Works very well, no complaints.Sound Quality: Signature mode is too bass-y, not for me, for sure. Balance mode does the job and it does it well.Call quality: THE BEST. Tested against Boat Rockers 255 Neo, Boult Audio X50, Dizo GoPods Neo, Realme T100.Build Quality: Buds good. Case lid, when closed, has a slight wobble to it. Everything else is upto the mark.One final remark would be in-ear fitting, and thus, noise isolation. I find these buds to provide noise isolation as good as Dizo Gopods Neo with its 25Db ANC mode on. It almost feels like they were custom made for my ears. But do keep in mind that this can and will differ from person to person.Thank you for your time and I hope I didn't waste it.Read more

Review Title: The Ultimate Disappointment: A Scathing Review of the Worst Earbuds Iĺve Ever Used
Review Description: The media could not be loaded.Let me preface this review by saying Iĺve tested countless earbuds over the years, ranging from budget-friendly options to premium audiophile gear. Never in my life have I encountered a product as fundamentally flawed, poorly designed, and outright infuriating as these earbuds. If youĺre considering purchasing these for gaming purposes, casual listening, or even as a backup pairŚSTOP. Save your money, your sanity, and your ears. From the abysmal mic functionality to the laughable sound quality, and the build quality that feels like it was engineered by someone whoĺs never held a pair of earbuds before, this product is a masterclass in failure. Letĺs break down why these earbuds are an insult to the very concept of functionality, clarity, and performance.1. Mic Functionality: A Joke for Gaming PurposesLetĺs start with the mic functionality, a feature thatĺs absolutely critical for gaming purposes or even casual Zoom calls. If youĺre buying earbuds to communicate with teammates, stream, or participate in online meetings, prepare to be humiliated. The microphone picks up every conceivable noise except your voice. Background chatter, the faint hum of your refrigerator, even your dog barking three rooms awayŚall of it is crystal clear. But your actual voice? Muffled, distorted, and buried under a layer of static that makes you sound like youĺre speaking through a tin can underwater.During a critical raid in my favorite MMO, my teammates repeatedly asked, ôAre you chewing gravel? Why does your mic sound like a broken walkie-talkie?ö When I switched to these earbudsĺ mic functionality, the response was unanimous: ôNever use these again.ö For gaming purposes, where clear communication is non-negotiable, this mic is a liability. Even after tweaking settings, updating drivers, and screaming into the microphone like a madman, the clarity never improved. Itĺs almost impressive how a company could engineer a microphone this bad.2. Gaming Purpose? More Like ôGame OveröSpeaking of gaming purposes, these earbuds fail on every front. Latency is so severe that actions in-gameŚlike gunfire or footstepsŚare delayed by a full second, making competitive play impossible. Imagine hearing an enemyĺs shotgun blast after youĺve already been respawned. The lack of clarity in spatial audio means you canĺt distinguish between sounds coming from your left or right, a death sentence in shooters or horror games.But the real kicker? The performance of these earbuds under load is laughable. After just 30 minutes of use, they overheat to the point of discomfort, and the audio begins to crackle like a campfire. For a product marketed toward gaming purposes, the complete disregard for basic functionality is staggering.3. Functionality: A Textbook Example of ôHow Not to Design EarbudsöThe functionality of these earbuds is so counterintuitive, it feels like a prank. The touch controls are hypersensitive yet unresponsiveŚa paradox that defies logic. Adjusting the volume? Prepare for a 50/50 chance of either muting the audio entirely or triggering your voice assistant. The companion app, which promises to ôenhance your experience,ö is a glitchy mess that crashes more often than a toddlerĺs toy car.Even basic functionality like pairing the earbuds to a device is a nightmare. They disconnect randomly, refuse to reconnect unless reset, and occasionally pair to the wrong device altogether (yes, my smart fridge now has audio privileges). For a product that claims to prioritize performance, the sheer lack of reliability is inexcusable.4. Clarity? More Like ôMuddy MessöIf you value clarity in your audio experience, steer clear. The sound profile is so unbalanced itĺs comical. Bass is overblown to the point of distortion, mids are nonexistent, and highs are shrill enough to shatter glass. Listening to music feels like being trapped in a nightclub with blown-out speakersŚexcept even nightclubs have better sound quality.For podcasts or audiobooks, the lack of clarity turns every narrator into a garbled mess. Voices blend into the background noise, and subtle details are drowned out by the earbudsĺ own hissing. Even at maximum volume, dialogue feels distant, as if the speaker is standing in another room. The sound quality isnĺt just badŚitĺs offensive.5. Performance: A Race to the BottomThe performance of these earbuds is a slap in the face to anyone whoĺs ever used a functional pair. The battery life, advertised as ôup to 8 hours,ö barely lasts two hours at 50% volume. And charging them? The case takes longer to power up than a medieval siege engine. Even the earbuds functionality during calls is a disaster. Every conversation becomes a game of ôGuess What I Said,ö with the other person constantly asking you to repeat yourself.Under stressŚlike multitasking between apps or running background downloadsŚthe performance nosedives further. Audio stutters, the mic cuts out, and the earbuds emit a high-pitched whine that could double as a dog whistle. For a product that claims to prioritize performance, itĺs clear no one tested these under real-world conditions.6. Earbuds Functionality: A Comedy of ErrorsThe earbuds functionality extends beyond poor sound and controls. The ergonomic design is a joke. They donĺt fit securely in any ear shape, requiring constant adjustments mid-use. During a jog, one earbud flew out and nearly became roadkill. The ôsweat-resistantö coating? A myth. After one gym session, the left earbud shorted out, emitting a smell akin to burnt plastic.Even simple tasks, like skipping a track or answering a call, require Herculean effort. The touch-sensitive areas are tiny and poorly mapped, leading to accidental hangs-ups or unintended playlist shuffles. For earbuds functionality thatĺs supposed to simplify your life, these add nothing but frustration.7. Sound Quality: An Assault on the SensesThe sound quality is where these earbuds truly cement their status as the worst on the market. The audio is flat, tinny, and devoid of depthŚlike listening to music through a cardboard tube. Genres that rely on nuance, like classical or jazz, are rendered unrecognizable. Rock and pop tracks lose their energy, with guitars sounding like kazoos and vocals like autotuned robots.Even for non-audiophiles, the sound quality is unbearable. Watching movies? Dialogue is drowned out by inconsistent volume levels, and action scenes lack punch. The lack of clarity and dynamic range makes every audio experience feel like a punishment.8. Build Quality: Cheap, Flimsy, and Downright DangerousThe build quality is a testament to corner-cutting. The plastic feels like it was salvaged from a dollar-store toy, with seams that creak under minimal pressure. The charging caseĺs lid wobbles like a loose tooth, and the earbuds themselves are so fragile, Iĺm afraid to breathe on them too hard.Within a week, the right earbudĺs mesh grill fell off, exposing the speaker to dust and debris. The charging pins corroded after minimal use, and the caseĺs hinge now emits a death rattle every time itĺs opened. For a product that costs more than a meal for two, the build quality is unforgivable.Conclusion: A Perfect Storm of FailureIn summary, these earbuds fail at every metric: mic functionality, gaming purposes, functionality, clarity, performance, earbuds functionality, sound quality, and build quality. Review Title: Great Sound, Comfortable Fit, and Seamless Samsung Integration
Review Description: Samsung Galaxy Buds 4 Pro ľ Great Upgrade from Oppo Enco X2 (Especially for Samsung Users)I upgraded from the Oppo Enco X2, which were honestly fantastic and very underrated earbuds.
